description_long: This object is a compact, tactical shotgun with a short barrel and a robust, utilitarian design. The body features a prominent ventilated heat shield above the barrel, likely for improved cooling during rapid fire. The grip is a pronounced pistol grip, ergonomically shaped for secure handling, and there is no visible full stock, making the weapon more maneuverable in close quarters. The receiver and barrel appear to be constructed from matte-finished metal, with some polymer components likely used in the grip and fore-end. The muzzle is short and squared off, with a visible front sight and a tubular magazine running beneath the barrel. The overall appearance suggests a modern, pump-action shotgun designed for urban tactical use.
description: A compact, tactical pump-action shotgun with a ventilated heat shield, pistol grip, and short barrel, finished in matte black metal and polymer.
description_view_0: Front view showing the short, squared muzzle and the alignment of the barrel and magazine tube.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the ventilated heat shield, pistol grip, trigger guard, and the overall compact profile.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the back of the receiver and the grip, with minimal stock visible.
description_view_3: Opposite side view showing the same tactical features, including the heat shield and grip, from the other side.
attribute_barrel_configuration: single-barrel with a ventilated heat shield and short barrel length
attribute_trigger_guard_design: rounded, medium-sized trigger guard with a smooth contour and no visible finger grooves
